[RE: Welcome to Together Loans Credit Builder!] Hi RONALD,

Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Together Loans CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $3000, on 03/18/2026. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder' email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: https://togetherloans.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can still help you access funds of up to $10,000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.

Many thanks,
Ben, Together Loans

[RE:Welcome to Together Loans Credit Builder!] Hi RONALD,

Thanks for reaching out to us, we're more than happy to help you with any queries and questions that you have. 

We do have a phone number, +1 470 435 6300, this goes to our 24/7 interactive call handler. This service will give you live updates about your application, and how it is progressing through to payout! Calling this number allows us to give you instant information and saves you long waits to speak to a person.

We also have a really helpful section on our website with answers to our frequently asked questions: https://togetherloans.com/Help/Contact

If you question isn't answered by either of these services, you're welcome to reply to this email explaining your problem in detail and someone will get back to you as soon as possible. 

Thanks,
Jade@Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0612110301012627061711] Hi RONALD,
I attempted to call you, as you requested. Unfortunately, I was not able to receive an answer. 
 
CreditBuilder is an optional credit building loan product that customers can sign up to. Customers make a $5 payment each month, over 1-2 years, which we report back to their Credit File as monthly payments against a small loan ($368) Please note that this is not a loan that is paid out to you directly. This loan is held on your behalf by a corporate guarantor we assign for you. This way, you get the opportunity to build your credit score without the stress of a big financial burden since you're not held responsible for the loan principal.

Each time you make a $5 payment, it's recorded on your credit history, which can help increase your credit score over time. If you decide to stop the payments before the loan term is completed or once you've completed the term, we mark the CreditBuilder loan as 'paid in full'. This means you never have to worry about missed payments negatively affecting your credit score either.

The aim of CreditBuilder is to support you in improving your credit score without taking on a heavy financial burden. When you feel ready to take a bigger step, we're here to help you access larger amounts - up to $10,000 with the help of a co-signer - and at that point, your CreditBuilder loan would be fully settled.

For more details, check out the CreditBuilder program on our website: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: https://togetherloans.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

Please respond to this message with your questions and a member of our team will be more than happy to assist you. If you still prefer a call back, please let me know a good timeframe to reach you and we will try our best to call you during that time.
Thanks, Together Loans

Hi RONALD,
I attempted to call you, as you requested. Unfortunately, I was not able to receive an answer. We offer two different products to help our customers achieve their financial goals:

1. Personal Loan ($3,000–$10,000) with a Cosigner:
All Together Loans loans require a cosigner. Unfortunately you will not be able to move ahead without a suitable cosigner. You can check this link to continue with your application and check the next step https://tcrdt.com/s/MYDVILDA

2. CreditBuilder:
Designed for customers who want to build credit without taking a full loan. You make a small $5 monthly payment for 24 months. These payments are reported to the credit bureaus, helping you strengthen your credit profile. You don’t receive loan funds, but if you stop paying early, the account is simply marked as paid in full—so it won’t hurt your credit.

You can learn more about CreditBuilder here: https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder

've sent you an important email regarding your CreditBuilder loan account. Please make sure to read it as soon as possible. If you have any questions, just let me know. Thanks, Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0600040323083308120823] Hi Theresa,
Thank you for taking the time to speak with me earlier.

I’m sorry to hear about your concerns, and I understand how upsetting this situation must feel. I’d like to reassure you that we have reviewed your application and we have not identified any charges associated with your account on our end. After you shared your concern, we deleted your bank information from our system.  Rest assured, your data is kept safe, used only in accordance with legal and regulatory guidelines, and never shared without your consent.

To clarify, providing bank details is part of our standard verification process. Additionally, all of our loans require a cosigner, which is why that request was made after your initial application. This is a normal part of our process and not related to any charges.

We apologize if you’ve been misinformed about our loans. It’s made clear on our websitehttps://togetherloans.com/ that we always require a cosigner, which was displayed before you applied and entered any details. The broker would’ve passed you to us if they thought we were the best fit for you, based on your circumstances. Although we can’t directly control the broker’s messaging, we're sure they would’ve had your best interests in mind. 

Having said that, we understand if our product is not something you wish to go ahead with at this time.

If you decide to apply again, you can use this link: https://togetherloans.com/

I can assure you that we do not scam or defraud our customers. We help hundreds of customers daily build up their credit score by offering cosigner loans and our Credit builder account. Our processes all follow applicable State and Federal laws and legislations that are applicable to our organization. Furthermore, Transform Credit Inc. DBA Together Loans is registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), with license number 1905055. You can find this information on our website:  https://togetherloans.com/Licenses. We have annual state audits in order to maintain our licenses, and if there were any significant issues, we would be unable to lend.

Thanks,
Together Loans
Hi, thanks for letting us know. We apologize, we were provided this contact by a customer. We've  removed this from our system; it may take a couple of days to take effect. Transform Credit

Hi Katherine, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/Y4UJ7OWT - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

Hi Pedro, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/TE5KPDS1 - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

Hi Pedro, a cosigner is someone who trusts you and agrees to make any repayments you don't. Most of our customers ask a friend or family member. Suitable cosigners have a credit score of 750+ and a history of paying bills on time. Homeowners don't usually need a score as high as 750 and anyone with over a 700 score has a fair chance of being accepted. If you're unsure if your cosigner will be accepted, ask them to complete their application online, and they will be informed if they are accepted. If you need to provide a new cosigner, use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/TE5KPDS1 Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0613031614000320111412] Hi Lynn,

When JEANINE's loan is ready to be paid out, the $3000 will be deposited into your bank account, so it's important we have these details. We do this to be absolutely sure that you are aware of the loan.
 
Additionally, by becoming a cosigner you are confirming that you trust JEANINE to make the loan repayments and that you agree to make any payments that they don't, and we can use these details as a backup method of payment if the account goes past due. 

To complete your loan application, you will need to provide us with a longstanding checking account (open at least 6 months), that is active, valid, in your name, and capable of ACH payments. 

Please note that you cannot use a prepaid account or joint bank account with the borrower, and online-only banks such as Chime and Cash App are less likely to be accepted from cosigners since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies.

To continue with your application, you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/FM6V1ATQ or let us know if you need more help.

Thanks, 
Together Loans

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0613031614000320111412] Hi JEANINE,

When the loan is ready to be paid out, the $3000 will be deposited into your cosigner's bank account for them to give to you, so it's important we have these details. 

We do this to be absolutely sure that the cosigner is aware of the loan. Additionally, because the cosigner agrees to make any payments that you don't, we can use these details as a backup method of payment if the account goes past due. 

Being a cosigner is a financial commitment and it is important that your cosigner fully understands their role and the responsibilities and obligations that come with becoming a co-signer. 

Your cosigner must add a longstanding checking account (open at least 6 months), that is active, valid, in their name, and capable of ACH payments. Please note that they cannot use a prepaid account or a joint account with you, and online-only banks such as Chime and Cash App are less likely to be accepted from cosigners since we cannot verify our customers' identities through these companies.

You can always check your application status here: https://tcrdt.com/s/WXJOQXN4

If you need any further help, please let us know.

Thanks, 
Together Loans

Hi Lynn. To continue with your loan application, you will need to provide us with a longstanding checking account (open at least 6 months), that is active, valid, in your name, and capable of ACH payments. Please note that you cannot use a prepaid account or joint account with the borrower, and online-only banks such as Chime are not accepted from cosigners since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies. To continue, you can use this link https://tcrdt.com/s/FM6V1ATQ or let us know if you need more help. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Lynn, you must have a bank account. We deposit the $3000 into your bank for you to give to JEANINE, to make sure you're fully aware of the loan. Also, as you agree to make any payments JEANINE doesn't, we use these details as a backup payment method ONLY if they don't pay. If you don't have a bank account you'll be unable to proceed, but if you do, you can continue here https://tcrdt.com/s/FM6V1ATQ Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Lynn,
Thanks for reaching out. To continue with your application, you will need to provide us with a longstanding ( at least 6 months to year old) checking account that is active, valid, in your name, and capable of ACH payments. 

Please note that you cannot use a prepaid account or joint account with the borrower, and online banks such as Chime,  PayPal, Go2, Venmo, Cash App, Varo etc., are less likely to be accepted since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies.

When JEANINE's loan is ready to be paid out, the $3000 will be deposited into your bank account, so it's important we have these details. We do this to be absolutely sure that you are aware of the loan. Additionally, by becoming a cosigner you are confirming that you trust JEANINE to make the loan repayments and that you agree to make any payments that they don't, and we can use these details as a backup method of payment if the account goes past due. 
Unfortunately, you won't be able to proceed without accepted bank information, even if other assets such as property or a vehicle are owned. Would you prefer to cancel your application as a cosigner so JEANINE can add a different cosigner? Thanks, Together Loans

Hi JEANINE,
Your cosigner must have an accepted bank account. We accept most banks and credit unions but not online banks such as Chime,  PayPal, Go2, Venmo, Cash App, Varo etc., we can't verify the cosigner’s identity through these companies. We also can’t accept joint accounts between borrowers and cosigners, or prepaid accounts. Your cosigner  must provide a longstanding ( at least 6 months to year old) checking account that is open, valid, in their name and capable of ACH payments.
When the loan is ready to be paid out, the $3000 will be deposited into your cosigner's bank account for them to give to you, so it's important we have these details.  We do this to be absolutely sure that the cosigner is aware of the loan. Additionally, because the cosigner agrees to make any payments that you don't, we can use these details as a backup method of payment if the account goes past due.  Unfortunately, they won't be able to proceed without accepted bank information. Would you like to cancel their application as a cosigner so you can add a different cosigner? To check your loan status, or to change your cosigner you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/WXJOQXN4 or let us know if you need more help.
Thank you, Together Loans

Hi Lynn,

Thank you for your message. We appreciate your trying to help Jeanine. To proceed with this loan, the funds must be deposited into your bank account as the cosigner. This ensures you are fully aware of the loan and agree to cover any payments if necessary.
Unfortunately, you won't be able to proceed without accepted bank information. We cannot send the money to your husband's bank account for security reason as your husband is not the cosigner but you are.  Would you prefer to cancel your application as a cosigner so JEANINE can add a different cosigner? Thanks, Together Loans

Hi JEANINE, we accept most banks but not online banks such as Chime and Cashapp, as we can't verify the cosigner's identity through these companies. We also can't accept joint accounts between borrowers and cosigners, or prepaid accounts. Lynn must provide an account that is open, valid, in their name and capable of ACH payments. Ask Lynn to add a suitable bank here: https://tcrdt.com/s/FM6V1ATQ Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0619082633030807071631] Hi Kim,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $5000, on <DateCreatedCb>. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder’ email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.
 
Many thanks, 
Michelle@TransformCredit

Hi Justinia, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/GVC5WZT0 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

Hi Justinia, a cosigner is someone who trusts you and agrees to make any repayments you don't. Most of our customers ask a friend or family member. Suitable cosigners have a credit score of 750+ and a history of paying bills on time. Homeowners don't usually need a score as high as 750 and anyone with over a 700 score has a fair chance of being accepted. If you're unsure if your cosigner will be accepted, ask them to complete their application online, and they will be informed if they are accepted. If you need to provide a new cosigner, use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/GVC5WZT0 Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Justinia, Thanks for reaching out. Together Loans is a cosigner-based lender, which means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. If you cannot find a cosigner, we can cancel your application if you prefer. You can apply again anytime! We can help you as soon as you find a cosigner. You can reply CANCEL, or use this link to continue when you are ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/UX5UYFV6 If you have any other questions, just let us know. Thanks, Together Loans

[ceeragillette] CA10c14807ba5b216717128aedb4422dd5

Hi Justinia, I attempted to call you, as you requested. Unfortunately, I was not able to receive an answer. We are a legitimate lender, incorporated in Delaware and based primarily out of Chicago. We have been lending for over 6 years, across 22 different states, all of which require licensing. You can find this information on our website: https://togetherloans.com/Licenses. We are currently applying for accreditation with the BBB which means we will be able to point customers to review our services there also, giving us a real rating. BBB has also investigated our business and was satisfied with our responses, which have been published on our page. [RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Rosalyn, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/W6VKYPPM - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

Hi Rosalyn, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/W6VKYPPM - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

[RE:Cancel subscription] Hi Isaiah,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3250 loan on 09/06/2025. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/GB65QN0J

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0625020906050710081502] Hi Joyell,

Thank you for reaching out.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not sign up for the CreditBuilder loan, as part of the application process. I can see that on December 12th, 2022, when you applied for this loan of $3000, you also reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, and you chose to sign up when clicked on “ok, set up my account”. I've attached a screenshot of this page to this email. 

After signing up, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Transform CreditBuilder!” email, confirming your account, and further explaining the benefits of the product you had opted into. I can see this was sent to you at December 12th, 2022 to joyellfisher@gmail.com. The email also provided you with your login link, so you could access your account and manage/cancel it, at any time, even before the first payment.

When you signed up for CreditBuilder, you took the first step in building your score. Each month you pay $5, we report these payments to the Credit Bureaus as interest against the loan that has been held on your behalf. These successful payments build your credit history, helping to improve your score. The great thing about CreditBuilder is that if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full and so missed payments can never undo the positive impact that your on-time payments have been making on your credit score. Our customers use CreditBuilder as a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. 

For the past 8 months, we’ve been sending you a payment reminder to your email address above, reminding you that your $5 CreditBuilder payment was due. These reminders again provided you with your login details, where you were able to manage or cancel your CreditBuilder, should you have decided you no longer wanted to benefit from the product.  We’ve also been reporting the CreditBuilder loan to Experian, demonstrating any payments you have made. As these on-time payments accumulate, they contribute positively to your overall credit score, paving the way for better credit opportunities in the future.

Based on the above, we will be unable to issue you a refund for the payments that you’ve made over the past 8 months. You chose to actively benefit from the product during this time, despite us being open and honest in all of our messages about your CreditBuilder account; including giving you the opportunity to close this at any point in time. 

However, I have canceled your Credit Builder account and this will now be marked as paid and settled on your credit report. Once again, please rest assured that you do not and never have owed us any money under this Credit Builder loan.

Many thanks,
Denzel@TransformCredit

interactions.body: Hello Laura, I'm sorry, but Chester wasn't a suitable cosigner at this time. A good cosigner has a credit score of 700+, and a good history of paying their bills on time. [RE:CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi Laura,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $6000 loan on 12/14/2025. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/IHFWOQCP

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Together Loans CreditBuilder

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0600020802090409012435] Hi Justin,

Thank you for contacting Transform Credit, your message is really important to us.

I can see that your application is active and you still have some tasks to finish before the loan is paid. Your current application contemplates a loan of $5500, over 60 months, paying back $198.74 monthly, but you can change these terms anytime before the loan is paid.

Transform Credit is a cosigner-based lender.  This means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. Normally, a cosigner is a friend or a family member who knows you (the borrower) and trusts that it is a good idea for you to borrow money. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a strong history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.

How does the application journey work? When you apply online, we will send you a unique link for your cosigner to register. As soon as we have everything we need from both of you, we’ll transfer the money. The money is transferred into your cosigner's account. This is to protect them, and us, against identity fraud, as transferring the money into their account ensures that they are fully aware of the loan.

You can check here more information about the application journey and common questions: https://transformcredit.com/faq 

To complete your application journey and check what is the next step, you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/UDJBWRP8

Any other questions, please let me know.

Have a nice day,
Eden@TransformCredit

interactions.body:
[RE:Missing Subject] Hi Angelia, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/4HSKA54W). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one TransformCredit loan at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $3000 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/4HSKA54W and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://transformcredit.com/faq.

Jade@TransformCredit 

P.S. I am ready to pay $3000 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else

[RE:Loan request Brian Mayerhofer] Hi Brian, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/XK7PXEPR). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one loan with Together Loans at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $7000 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/XK7PXEPR and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://https://togetherloans.com/faq.

Jade@Together Loans

P.S. I am ready to pay $7000 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else

Hi Joseorlando. Thank you for reaching out. We understand this situation has been disappointing. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. However, I can confirm that at this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages. Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into.  We appreciate your understanding. We can still pay out your $5000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/LVCWRUEQ Thanks, Transform Credit

hours, depending on the bank's processing time of the transfer to receive
the funds.

Today, Monday, June 3rd, 2024, I called TransformCredit in regards to the
loan stated above and I was on hold for over 20 mins. Any other time I
called, I was on hold for seconds before someone answered my call. I have
no knowledge of the loan's outcome and the feeling is unfair to know
apparently everyone knows what's going on but the person who initially
applied for the loan. I have no updates, I cannot leave a message, no one
is answering my calls or emailing me back, not even a text.

If the application was denied for whatever reason, like everyone else, I
would highly appreciate a reason, an explanation of the outcome and notice
as to what will happen to my personal information that was collected in
order to process my loan request. Including name, address, phone number,
SSN, routing and account numbers, debit card information... All of the
banking information, please.

TransformCredit has all of my personal information and three ways to reach
me at any time.

Thank you for your time and consideration,
Yazmin Pacheco Magallon.

[RE:Missing Subject] Hi Francisco, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/V1WJZIUT). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one TransformCredit loan at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $10000 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/V1WJZIUT and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://transformcredit.com/faq.

Jade@TransformCredit 

P.S. I am ready to pay $10000 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else
